title: sled-storage/journal: Define JournalEntry types and key encoding
description: Define JournalEntry enum per ADR-005 and key encoding helpers for journal tree. JournalEntry variants: Run, Sleep, WaitForSignal, GetState, SetState, Choice, Parallel, Complete, Error. Key format: invocation_id:seq.

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL encode journal keys as invocation_id:seq
THE SYSTEM SHALL serialize JournalEntry as JSON

postconditions: decode_journal_key parses invocation_id and seq correctly

happy: decode_journal_key parses key back to invocation_id and seq
error: decode_journal_key fails on invalid format (no colon)
decode_journal_key fails on invalid seq (not u32)
edge: ULID with special characters in invocation_id

phase_0: Read ADR-007 for key encoding format
phase_1: Write unit tests for encode/decode functions
phase_2: Define JournalEntry enum with all 9 variants per ADR-005

title: sled-storage/journal: Implement append_journal_entry function
description: Implement append_journal_entry that inserts a journal entry into the JOURNAL tree with key invocation_id:seq and calls flush_async after insert.

resolved: flush_async is called after insert for durability

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL append journal entry with invocation_id:seq key
THE SYSTEM SHALL flush after append for durability
unwanted: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT return before data is flushed

preconditions: db must be open with JOURNAL tree accessible
postconditions: Entry is persisted to sled and flushed

happy: append_journal_entry data can be retrieved with get_journal_entry

phase_1: Write test that appends then retrieves same entry

title: sled-storage/journal: Implement get_journal_entry function
description: Implement get_journal_entry that retrieves a single journal entry by invocation_id and seq. Returns Option<JournalEntry>.

resolved: Returns Option<JournalEntry>: Some(entry) if found, None if not
Uses get with encoded key on journal tree

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL return journal entry if it exists
THE SYSTEM SHALL return None if entry does not exist

happy: get_journal_entry returns Some for existing entry
get_journal_entry returns None for missing seq
edge: get_journal_entry for different invocation_id returns None

phase_1: Write test for existing and non-existing entries
phase_2: Use tree.get() and deserialize from JSON

title: sled-storage/journal: Implement get_all_journal_entries function
description: Implement get_all_journal_entries that retrieves all journal entries for an invocation using prefix scan. Returns Vec<(seq, JournalEntry)> sorted by seq ascending.

resolved: Uses scan_prefix with invocation_id + ":" as prefix
Returns empty vec if no entries

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL return all journal entries for invocation_id
THE SYSTEM SHALL return entries sorted by seq ascending

postconditions: All entries for invocation_id are returned
Entries are sorted by seq ascending

happy: get_all_journal_entries returns all entries for invocation
Entries are sorted by seq ascending
edge: Multiple entries with non-sequential seq values

phase_1: Write test that appends 5 entries and retrieves them in order

title: sled-storage/journal: Implement get_journal_length function
description: Implement get_journal_length that returns the count of journal entries for an invocation. Returns u32.

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL return count of journal entries for invocation_id

happy: get_journal_length returns correct count after N appends

phase_1: Write test that verifies count matches actual entries
phase_2: Use scan_prefix and count entries via .count()

title: sled-storage/journal: Implement delete_journal function
description: Implement delete_journal that removes all journal entries for an invocation. Uses scan_prefix to find all entries then removes them.

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL delete all journal entries for invocation_id

postconditions: All entries for invocation_id are deleted

happy: delete_journal removes all entries for invocation

phase_1: Write test that appends, deletes, and verifies empty
phase_2: Use scan_prefix to find all keys

title: sled-storage/journal: Add comprehensive unit tests
description: Add comprehensive unit tests for all journal functions using temp directory for sled database isolation.

happy: Test full workflow: append -> get_one -> get_all -> get_length -> delete

phase_1: Write tests for encode_journal_key and decode_journal_key
phase_2: Write integration tests for all public functions
